candidates.text:
Step 1: Turn off the water supply valve located near the base of the old toilet and flush the toilet to drain the tank and bowl. Use a sponge or old towels to soak up any remaining water in the bowl.
***
Step 2: Disconnect the water supply line from the bottom of the toilet tank by unscrewing the coupling nut with a wrench or adjustable pliers.
***
Step 3: Remove the bolts or caps covering the closet bolts at the base of the toilet. If they are capped, pry them off carefully. Then, rock the toilet gently side to side to break the wax seal, and lift the toilet straight up to remove it.
***
Step 4: Scrape away the old wax seal from the floor flange using a putty knife or scraper. Inspect the flange for damage and ensure it is level and securely fastened to the floor; replace it if necessary.
***
Step 5: Clean the flange and surrounding floor area thoroughly, removing any debris or old wax residue, and place a new wax ring (or wax-free seal) onto the flange, aligning the horn with the drain opening.

Let me know how it works. I can give you next steps when you finish all steps above.

Step 1: Turn off the water supply at the shut‑off valve near the existing toilet, then flush the tank and bowl to drain most of the water; use a sponge or bucket to remove any remaining water from the tank and bowl.  
***
Step 2: Disconnect the supply line from the bottom of the old toilet and remove the two bolts (or the flange bolts) that hold the toilet to the floor; gently rock the toilet back and forth to break the wax seal, then lift it off and set it aside on old towels or a drop cloth.  
***
Step 3: Scrape away the old wax ring and any debris from the floor flange; inspect the flange for damage or rot, and if needed, replace or repair the flange so it sits level with or slightly above the finished floor.  
***
Step 4: Clean the flange and surrounding floor area; apply a new wax (or wax‑with‑horn) ring to the underside of the new toilet bowl, making sure the horn (if present) points down toward the drain opening.  
***
Step 5: Lower the new toilet straight onto the flange, aligning the bolt holes with the flange bolts, and press down firmly to compress the wax ring and create a seal; do not rock the toilet after the seal is made.  

Let me know how it works. I can give you next steps when you finish all steps above.

Step 1: Turn off the water supply to the toilet by closing the shut-off valve located near the floor, then flush the toilet to empty the tank and bowl.
***
Step 2: Disconnect the flexible water supply line from the bottom of the toilet tank using a wrench.
***
Step 3: Carefully rock the toilet back and forth to break the seal of the wax ring, then lift the toilet off the floor and set it aside on an old towel or cardboard.
***
Step 4: Remove the old wax ring and clean the flange and floor area thoroughly to ensure a proper seal for the new toilet.
***
Step 5: Inspect the toilet flange for damage or rust; if it is damaged, repair or replace it before proceeding.

Let me know how it works. I can give you next steps when you finish all steps above.

Step 1: Turn off the water supply valve located on the wall behind or near the toilet, then flush the toilet to drain the tank and bowl. Use a sponge or old towels to soak up any remaining water in the bowl.  
***
Step 2: Remove the nuts or bolts securing the toilet to the floor by unscrewing the caps on the base. If the bolts are corroded, use a wrench or bolt extractor to loosen them.  
***
Step 3: Break the wax seal by rocking the toilet gently side-to-side, then lift the toilet straight up and set it aside on a drop cloth or old towels to avoid damaging the floor.  
***
Step 4: Scrape off the old wax seal from the flange using a putty knife, and inspect the flange for cracks or damage. If needed, replace the flange or secure it to the floor with new screws.  
***
Step 5: Place a new wax ring (or wax-free seal) onto the flange, aligning it with the drain hole, then carefully lower the toilet straight down onto the flange to compress the seal evenly.  

Let me know how it works. I can give you next steps when you finish all steps above.
